commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content:
Howbeit Jesus spake of his death, &c. Because they took the "sleepeth" simply, not symbolically, of death, as Christ
meant it.

commentaries.author: Cyril of Alexandria
commentaries.content: They, not understanding the force of the words, thought that Jesus spake of taking rest in sleep, which when sick men can do, they generally experience refreshment; wherefore the disciples say: "It is not worth while to go and disturb Lazarus from his sleep, for it does not benefit a sick man to awake him out of sleep." And this they said, wishing to hinder Him from the journey by remarking that it was not meet to go into the midst of those murderers for the sake of doing something which would produce no good result.
